document.title: Using tkintertester with lionscliapp
document.purpose: Define the canonical integration pattern between lionscliapp and tkintertester while preserving lifecycle separation and unified execution.

core_principle.layer_relationship: Systems operate at different layers without lifecycle conflict.

rules: tkintertester is invoked from inside the normal run command
Harness behavior is controlled via runtime flags
The preferred hosted pattern is to always invoke harness.run_host(app_entry, flags) from the GUI command
Tkinter must not start during declaration phase

command_layout.command: ui (or the app's one canonical GUI command)
command_layout.description: A single unified GUI command executes the application and optional tests.

runtime.tests.show.meaning: Show test results within tkinter after tests complete.

runtime.tests.isolated.meaning: Required for Tk test runs in lionscliapp. Prevents accidental testing against a live data environment.

integration_pattern.description: Unified GUI command that conditionally registers tests and always invokes tkintertester through run_host().
why_this_pattern_is_favored: One real hosted runtime path is used for both tests and normal GUI execution.
Tests do not require a separate command or alternate entrypoint.
The same app_entry function constructs the live UI in both modes.
lionscliapp completes its own setup before tkintertester takes over the Tk lifecycle.

application_lifecycle_functions.app_entry: Responsible for building UI, and GUI app initialization (post-CLI setup).
application_lifecycle_functions.app_reset: Responsible for destroying UI, and resetting state to the start state.
reset_guidance: Destroy any live toplevel or root-owned surfaces.
Clear widget dictionaries or mutable app references used by tests.
Restore the same baseline state that app_entry expects.

host_model.description: tkintertester owns the Tk lifecycle once run_host() is invoked.

attach_mode.description: Optional capability allowing tests to run against an already-running application.

lionscliapp: enforces Tk test isolation policy when configured

hard_rules: Do not start Tk during declaration phase.
Only one Tk mainloop per process.
For lionscliapp Tk commands, if runtime.tests.enabled is true, runtime.tests.isolated should also be true and the invocation should use an isolated project-dir/runtime target.
invariants: Exactly one canonical GUI command exists for execution.
Harness is always invoked through the application's normal GUI command.
Tests modify behavior but do not change entrypoint.
The favored pattern is to always invoke run_host() from the GUI command, whether or not tests are enabled.
philosophy: tkintertester decides what happens inside Tk.

build_tkintertester_flags.meaning: Translate runtime.tests.show and runtime.tests.exit into tkintertester host flags.

tests_enabled.meaning: Interpret runtime.tests.enabled as a boolean-like runtime flag.

tk_single_instance_note.summary: When used with lionscliapp's Tk single-instance runtime, tests should still go through the same GUI command path. The host framework decides whether the invocation is a normal summonable GUI run or an isolated test run.
